user_query:
I want a small, event-triggered folder automation that processes new support ticket batches and produces concise team-ready outputs. Please implement a script or service that watches (or can be run against) the folder input/inbox for *.jsonl files, using the two provided example files to demonstrate it works.

When a new batch file appears in input/inbox:
1) For each ticket record, rewrite the message into a concise, friendly summary no longer than 140 characters. Keep key facts like error codes, page/component, and timestamps when relevant; remove stack traces, ALL-CAPS emphasis, and ranty phrasing. The rewritten text should be clear, neutral, and brief.
2) Compute per-batch statistics over the original messages and metadata:
   - total_tickets
   - tickets_by_severity (counts per severity value)
   - avg_message_length_chars (original message text)
   - avg_message_length_words (original message text)
   - earliest_created_at and latest_created_at (ISO timestamps from created_at)
   - unique_reporters (count of distinct reporter values)
3) Write the following outputs for each processed batch (where <basename> is the input filename without extension):
   - out/rewritten/<basename>.rewrites.jsonl: one JSON object per line with fields [id, severity, reporter, created_at, original_message, rewritten_message].
   - out/metrics/<basename>.summary.json: a single JSON object containing exactly the statistics listed in step 2.
   - out/notifications/<basename>.team_update.txt: a friendly, single-paragraph summary message no longer than 200 characters that mentions the total tickets, counts by severity, and the batch time range (earliest_created_at–latest_created_at).
   - out/state/processed_batches.json: a JSON array of processed basenames with ISO timestamps of when your automation handled them (useful for idempotency so re-runs skip already processed batches).

Please process the two provided example files in input/inbox and generate the corresponding outputs under out/. The automation should be idempotent: re-running it should not duplicate work for files already listed in out/state/processed_batches.json. Keep the implementation self-contained and runnable locally without external services.

Input format notes: each input file is JSONL with one ticket per line. Each ticket has fields id, severity, message, created_at (ISO 8601), and reporter.

Deliverables: create all four output artifacts per input batch at the exact paths described so I can verify them against the inputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
